stemHtml: Regarding the speeds V<sub>NO</sub>, V<sub>MO</sub> and V<sub>NE</sub>, which of the following answers is correct?

options.a: V<sub>NO</sub> is the maximum normal operating airspeed for an aeroplane certified under CS-25. It can only be exceeded in smooth air.
options.b: V<sub>MO</sub> is the maximum operating speed permitted in normal conditions for an aeroplane certified under CS-25. It may be exceeded by a maximum of 10% in an emergency descent.
options.c: For an aeroplane certified under CS-25, V<sub>MO</sub> is the maximum speed that must not be deliberately exceeded in any regime, unless a higher speed has been authorised for a particular flight.
options.d: For an aeroplane certified under CS-23, V<sub>NE</sub> is the maximum speed to be flown with the flaps extended.
